name: Adelphi Theatre
searchPreviewText: Popular theatre first built as the Sans Pareil, renamed Adelphi in 1820.

name: Admiralty
searchPreviewText: Naval business and patronage are directed from these offices on Whitehall.

name: Albany
searchPreviewText: Fashionable bachelor chambers stand just off Piccadilly.

name: Aldersgate Street Station
searchPreviewText: Serves General Post Office, St Paul's, Christ's Hospital, Smithfield, Goswell Road, Charterhouse Square, and Snow Hill.

name: Aldgate Station
searchPreviewText: The Metropolitan Railway's eastern station serves Leadenhall Street, Fenchurch Street, Mark Lane, Eastcheap, the docks, Whitechapel, and Commercial Road.

name: Alexandra Mansions
searchPreviewText: Private residential family club for visitors to town, four minutes from South Kensington station.

name: Alhambra Theatre
searchPreviewText: A handsome theatre and music hall, chiefly devoted to burlesque and comic opera.

name: Argentine Republic Consulate
searchPreviewText: La Plata / Argentine Republic consulate.

name: Austria-Hungary Consulate
searchPreviewText: Austro-Hungarian consular office.

name: Austria-Hungary Embassy
searchPreviewText: Embassy in Belgrave Square.

name: Baker Street Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Regent's Park West, Madame Tussaud's, Baker Street Bazaar, Portman Square, and Manchester Square.

name: Bank of England
searchPreviewText: Core City financial institution near Royal Exchange and Mansion House.

name: Barclay & Son
searchPreviewText: Lamps, colza oil, kerosene oil, wax, sperm, and paraffin candles.

name: Battersea Park
searchPreviewText: A broad riverside park of 180 acres, drained, planted, and admired for its sub-tropical garden.

name: Bayswater Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Kensington Gardens, Kensington Palace Gardens, Princes Square, Moscow Road, synagogue, and Greek Church.

name: Belgium Consulate
address: 11 Bury Court, St Mary Axe, London
searchPreviewText: Belgian consular office.

name: Belgium Embassy
searchPreviewText: Embassy near Victoria.

name: Bethlem Hospital
searchPreviewText: Bedlam now stands at Lambeth Road, with patients given work, music, games, and exercise.

name: Bethnal Green Museum
searchPreviewText: Museum removed from South Kensington to the East End.

name: Billingsgate Fish Market
searchPreviewText: Red-brick fish market below London Bridge; wholesale trading over by morning.

name: Bishopsgate
searchPreviewText: Great Eastern Railway access to Bishopsgate, Shoreditch, Victoria Park, Bank, Royal Exchange, and London Bridge.

name: Blackfriars Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Blackfriars Bridge and Road, Farringdon Street, Fleet Street, Ludgate Hill, St Paul's, Queen Victoria Street, and Cheapside.

name: Booksellers Row
searchPreviewText: Synonymous with London's underground print culture, evolving from a mart for cheap broadside ballads and music sheets to a focal point for radical publishing and erotica.

name: Borough Market
searchPreviewText: Vegetable and fruit market adjacent to St Saviour's.

name: Brazil Consulate
searchPreviewText: Brazilian consular office.

name: Brazil Embassy
searchPreviewText: Embassy near Victoria.

name: Brown and Green
searchPreviewText: Prize medal kitcheners and cooking stoves, advertised as saving coal and curing smoky chimneys.

name: Buckingham Palace
searchPreviewText: The royal palace stands west of St James's Park on the route from Pall Mall to Pimlico.

name: Burlington Arcade
searchPreviewText: A fashionable covered arcade runs beside Burlington House on Piccadilly.

name: Burlington House
searchPreviewText: Learned societies and art exhibition centre.

name: Canada Agency-General
searchPreviewText: Dominion of Canada Agency-General.

name: Cannon Street Hotel
searchPreviewText: A railway hotel attached to the Cannon Street terminus.

name: Cannon Street Station
searchPreviewText: A South-Eastern Railway city terminus stands in Cannon Street, close to Mansion House.

name: Cardinal & Harford
searchPreviewText: Importers of Turkey, Persian, and Oriental carpets, rugs, and matting.

name: Charing Cross Underground Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Thames Embankment, South Eastern Railway, Society of Arts, Strand, Trafalgar Square, National Gallery, theatres, Haymarket, Pall Mall, and Waterloo Place.

name: Chile Consulate
address: Gresham House, Old Broad Street, London
searchPreviewText: Chilean consular office.

name: Christ's Hospital
searchPreviewText: Famous school near Newgate Street and Cheapside.

name: Civil Service Supply Association
searchPreviewText: Chief store of the Civil Service Supply Association.

name: Cleopatra's Needle
searchPreviewText: A granite Egyptian obelisk, seventy feet high, newly raised on the Thames Embankment.

name: Coal Exchange
searchPreviewText: Fine building decorated with fossil plant motifs from coal formations.

name: Colombia Consulate
searchPreviewText: United States of Colombia consular office.

name: Colonial Office
searchPreviewText: Office of the Secretary of State for Britain's colonies.

name: Columbia Market
searchPreviewText: Built at Baroness Burdett Coutts's expense for the East-end poor.

name: Corn Exchange
searchPreviewText: Mark Lane's exchange draws the corn trade into the eastern City.

name: Costa Rica Consulate
searchPreviewText: Costa Rican consular office.

name: Coutts's and Co. Bank
searchPreviewText: A sober Strand bank whose plain frontage hides deep and valuable cellarage.

name: Covent Garden Market
searchPreviewText: Famous fruit, vegetable, and flower market in the West End.

name: Cranwell & Co.
searchPreviewText: Turkey and Eastern Carpet Depot, importers.

name: Cremorne Gardens
searchPreviewText: The famous Chelsea pleasure gardens have closed, but the riverside still carries their reputation.

name: Custom House
searchPreviewText: River-facing customs institution, central to London's port trade.

name: David Bogue
searchPreviewText: Publisher of Health Primers and Half-Hour Volumes.

name: Debain & Co.
searchPreviewText: Harmoniums, organophones, mechanical pianos, and pianos; London representative at Rathbone Place.

name: Denmark Consulate
searchPreviewText: Danish consular office.

name: Denmark Embassy
searchPreviewText: Danish embassy in Wimpole Street.

name: Earl's Court Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Earl's Court Road and westward districts.

name: Egyptian Hall
searchPreviewText: A Piccadilly exhibition hall known for public curiosities and entertainments.

name: Elephant and Castle
searchPreviewText: Mail coaches, omnibuses, and south London roads converge at this busy rendezvous.

name: Exeter Hall
searchPreviewText: Exeter Hall is the venue for many great gatherings of activists for various causes, most notably the anti-slavery movement and the meeting of the Anti–Corn Law League. It is also the headquarters of the Young Men's Christian Association.

name: Farringdon Street Station
searchPreviewText: Serves meat market, Farringdon Market, Clerkenwell, Hatton Garden, Holborn Viaduct, Gray's Inn, Ludgate Hill, Fleet Street, Chancery Lane, Temple, Strand, and Blackfriars.

name: Fenchurch Street Station
searchPreviewText: Railway terminus for eastward routes.

name: Finchley Road Station
searchPreviewText: A Metropolitan extension station on the north-western line toward Willesden.

name: Floral Hall
searchPreviewText: Associated with the Covent Garden market and theatre district.

name: France Consulate
searchPreviewText: French consular office.

name: France Embassy
address: Albert Gate House, Hyde Park, London
searchPreviewText: French embassy at Albert Gate House.

name: Garrick Club
searchPreviewText: A theatrical club with a notable collection of portraits of actors and stage figures.

name: General Post Office
searchPreviewText: Major postal institution near St Paul's and Cheapside.

name: German Empire Consulate
searchPreviewText: German consular office.

name: German Empire Embassy
searchPreviewText: German embassy by The Mall.

name: Gower Street Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Tottenham Court Road, Hampstead Road, Euston, Russell Square, British Museum, Bloomsbury, Camden Town, Kentish Town, and theatres.

name: Great Western Royal Hotel
searchPreviewText: The Great Western Hotel stands opposite Paddington Station on Praed Street.

name: Greece Consulate
searchPreviewText: Greek consular office.

name: Greece Embassy
searchPreviewText: Greek embassy in Pall Mall.

name: Grosvenor Hotel
searchPreviewText: A large railway hotel serves travellers arriving at Victoria.

name: Guatemala Consulate
searchPreviewText: Guatemalan consular office.

name: Guildhall
searchPreviewText: Seat of City government with Gog and Magog and the Guildhall Museum.

name: Guy's Hospital
searchPreviewText: Founded by Thomas Guy, bookseller and speculator.

name: H. Lamplough
searchPreviewText: Consulting chemist advertising Pyretic Saline and lime juice syrup.

name: H. Schweitzer & Co.
searchPreviewText: Sole proprietors of Cocoatina, advertised as soluble cocoa or chocolate powder.

name: Hawaiian Islands Consulate
searchPreviewText: Hawaiian Islands consular office.

name: Horns Tavern
searchPreviewText: A Kennington tavern and assembly-room known for large political and public meetings.

name: India Office
searchPreviewText: Office regulating the affairs of the Indian Empire.

name: Italy Consulate
searchPreviewText: Italian consular office.

name: Italy Embassy
searchPreviewText: Italian embassy in Queen's Gate.

name: J. H. Steward
searchPreviewText: Optician to British and foreign governments; binocular, opera, field, tourist, and marine glasses.

name: James How & Co.
searchPreviewText: Chemical and philosophical instrument makers to government; microscopes, telescopes, lanterns, and apparatus.

name: Kensington High Street Station
searchPreviewText: Near vestry hall and old church; serves Pro-Cathedral, Carmelite Church, Holland House, and Phillimore Gardens.

name: Kensington Palace
searchPreviewText: Served by Bayswater / Kensington area entries.

name: King's College London
searchPreviewText: College, hospital, and school stand together on the busy eastward run of the Strand.

name: King's Cross Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Pentonville, Islington, Agricultural Hall, Holloway, Barnsbury, Gray's Inn Road, Foundling Hospital, Great Northern and Midland Railways.

name: Lambeth Palace
searchPreviewText: Residence of the Archbishops of Canterbury for six and a half centuries.

name: Langham Hotel
searchPreviewText: Served by Portland Road station; major hotel near Regent Street.

name: Leadenhall Market
searchPreviewText: Historic market serving the City.

name: Leicester Square
searchPreviewText: Foreign exiles, theatres, clubs, and lodging houses give the square a restless continental air.

name: Liberia Consulate
address: 18 Pinners' Hall, Old Broad Street, London
searchPreviewText: Liberian consular office.

name: Lloyd's of London
searchPreviewText: Merchants and insurers gather here in the commercial heart of the City.

name: London Bridge Station
searchPreviewText: Railway terminus serving London Bridge and the Borough.

name: London Docks
searchPreviewText: Large dock system with tobacco warehouses, wine vaults, wool, spices, tea, and drugs.

name: Mansion House
searchPreviewText: Lord Mayor's official residence near Bank and Royal Exchange.

name: Marlborough House
searchPreviewText: West End royal residence near St James's Palace.

name: Marlborough Road Station
searchPreviewText: Branch station for Eyre Arms, Portland Town, and Acacia Road.

name: Marshalsea Prison site
searchPreviewText: The old Marshalsea has gone, but its memory lingers in Borough High Street.

name: Mexico Consulate
address: 4 Adam's Court, Old Broad Street, London
searchPreviewText: Mexican consular office.

name: Midland Grand Hotel
searchPreviewText: A grand railway hotel rises above the Midland terminus on Euston Road.

name: Monument
searchPreviewText: Monument to the Great Fire; prominent City landmark.

name: Moorgate Street Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Finsbury, Cheapside, Gresham Street, Guildhall, Mansion House, Bank, Cornhill, Lombard Street, and London Bridge.

name: Mrs Addley Bourne
searchPreviewText: Ladies' outfitter, corset and baby linen manufacturer; bridal trousseaux and layettes.

name: National Gallery
searchPreviewText: The old King's Mews has given way to a national picture gallery facing Trafalgar Square.

name: Natural History Museum
searchPreviewText: A major museum of natural history anchors the South Kensington museum district.

name: Netherlands Consulate
searchPreviewText: Netherlands consular office.

name: Netherlands Embassy
searchPreviewText: Embassy near Victoria.

name: Newgate Prison
searchPreviewText: A grim prison beside the Old Bailey, long bound to executions and criminal trials.

name: Nicaragua Consulate
searchPreviewText: Nicaraguan consular office.

name: North British and Mercantile Insurance Company
searchPreviewText: Fire and life insurance company with London and Edinburgh head offices.

name: Notting Hill Gate Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Camden Hill, Pembridge Square, Holland Park, Pembridge Gardens, Linden Grove, and Ladbroke Road.

name: Paddington Station
searchPreviewText: Opposite the Great Western Hotel; serves Great Western Railway, Hyde Park, Eastbourne Terrace, Westbourne Terrace, Gloucester Square, and Craven Hill.

name: Peru Consulate
searchPreviewText: Peruvian consular office.

name: Portland Road Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Albany Street, Regent's Park, St George's Hall, Polytechnic, Langham Hotel, Regent Street, and Oxford Street.

name: Portugal Consulate
searchPreviewText: Portuguese consular office.

name: Portugal Embassy
searchPreviewText: Portuguese embassy near Portman Square.

name: Rent Guarantee Society Ltd
searchPreviewText: Collects rents, tithes, mortgage interest, dividends, and manages property.

name: Royal Albert Hall
searchPreviewText: A great South Kensington hall near the Horticultural Gardens and the Albert Memorial.

name: Royal Botanic Gardens
searchPreviewText: Botanical gardens and society grounds occupy part of Regent's Park.

name: Royal Chelsea Hospital
searchPreviewText: Wren-built retreat for old soldiers, traditionally linked with Nell Gwynne.

name: Royal College of Physicians
searchPreviewText: Portraits and relics of eminent physicians are kept in this learned college by Trafalgar Square.

name: Royal Courts of Justice
searchPreviewText: New law courts area serving Strand, Fleet Street, and the Inns of Court.

name: Royal Exchange
searchPreviewText: Commercial exchange at the heart of the City.

name: Royal Mint
searchPreviewText: The coinage of the realm is struck near Tower Hill and the old fortress.

name: Russia Consulate
searchPreviewText: Russian consular office.

name: Russia Embassy
searchPreviewText: Russian embassy in Belgrave Square.

name: Salvador Consulate
searchPreviewText: Salvador Republic consular office.

name: Samaritan Free Hospital for Women and Children
searchPreviewText: Free hospital for women and children with in-patient and out-patient departments.

name: San Domingo Consulate
searchPreviewText: San Domingo consular office.

name: Scotland Yard
searchPreviewText: The Metropolitan Police keep their headquarters here, amid older memories of Milton, Wren, and court officials.

name: Siam Consulate
searchPreviewText: Siamese consular office.

name: Sloane Square Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Sloane Street, East Chelsea, Chelsea Hospital, Battersea Park, King's Road, Royal Court Theatre, Prince's Cricket Ground, and Eaton Square.

name: Smithfield Meat Market
searchPreviewText: Metropolitan meat market; cattle market had moved to Copenhagen Fields.

name: Somerset House
searchPreviewText: Government offices and Inland Revenue associations in the Strand.

name: South Australia Agency-General
address: 8 Victoria Chambers, Victoria Street, Westminster, London
searchPreviewText: Agency-General for South Australia.

name: South Kensington Museum
searchPreviewText: Served by South Kensington station; major museum quarter.

name: South Kensington Station
searchPreviewText: Serves South Kensington Museum, Oratory, Horticultural Gardens, Royal Albert Hall, Albert Memorial, Brompton Road, Fulham Road, Cromwell Road, Chelsea West, and Thurloe Square.

name: Spain Consulate
searchPreviewText: Spanish consular office.

name: Spain Embassy
address: 12 Queen's Gate Place, South Kensington, London
searchPreviewText: Spanish embassy in Queen's Gate Place.

name: St Bride's Church
searchPreviewText: A notable City church tucked just south of Fleet Street and its printers.

name: St George's Roman Catholic Cathedral
searchPreviewText: Built by A. W. Pugin in 1848.

name: St James's Palace
searchPreviewText: Palace and court quarter in the Pall Mall route.

name: St James's Park Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Birdcage Walk, Royal Aquarium, Victoria Street, Army and Navy Stores, Westminster, Palace Hotel, Broad Sanctuary, Dean's Yard, and Westminster Hospital.

name: St John's Wood Road Station
searchPreviewText: Branch station for Lord's Cricket Ground, Park Road, Regent's Park, Primrose Hill, and Wellington Road.

name: St Katherine's Docks
searchPreviewText: One of London's major docks, nearest London Bridge.

name: St Martin-in-the-Fields
searchPreviewText: A handsome church in florid Roman or Italian style with a western Corinthian portico.

name: St Marylebone Female Protection Society
searchPreviewText: Founded in 1838 to shelter and train young women and mothers in distress.

name: St Paul's Cathedral
searchPreviewText: Wren's great cathedral dominates the City with its dome, monuments, and Whispering Gallery.

name: St Saviour's Church
searchPreviewText: Southwark's great old church rivals Westminster Abbey for historical interest.

name: St Thomas's Hospital
searchPreviewText: A charitable foundation now serving as one of London's great hospitals by Westminster Bridge.

name: Surrey Theatre
searchPreviewText: Theatre at the end of Great Surrey Street, near five-road junction.

name: Sweden and Norway Consulate
searchPreviewText: Swedish and Norwegian consular office.

name: Sweden and Norway Embassy
searchPreviewText: Embassy near Berkeley Square.

name: Switzerland Consulate
searchPreviewText: Swiss consular office.

name: Temple Church
searchPreviewText: The church of the Temple stands among the lawyers' courts and gardens between Strand and Fleet Street.

name: Temple Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Inns of Court, east Strand, new law courts, and Fleet Street.

name: Thames Tunnel
searchPreviewText: Brunel's tunnel beneath the Thames now carries East London Railway traffic between Wapping and Rotherhithe.

name: Thomas Aldred
searchPreviewText: Archery manufacturer to the Royal Family and crowned heads of Europe.

name: Tower of London
searchPreviewText: Best river view of Traitor's Gate; fortress and prison landmark.

name: Turkey Consulate
address: 55 to 58 Ethelburga House, 70 and 71 Bishopsgate Street Within, London
searchPreviewText: Turkish consular office in Ethelburga House.

name: Turkey Embassy
searchPreviewText: Turkish embassy in Bryanston Square.

name: United States Consulate
searchPreviewText: The United States keeps its City consular office in Old Broad Street, separate from the Westbourne Place embassy.

name: United States Embassy
searchPreviewText: US embassy address in west London.

name: Uruguay Consulate
searchPreviewText: Republic of Uruguay / Monte Video consular address.

name: Vaudeville Theatre
searchPreviewText: Scene of very popular comedies by H. J. Byron.

name: Venezuela Consulate
searchPreviewText: Venezuelan consular office.

name: Victoria Palace Theatre
searchPreviewText: A large theatre famous for pronounced melodramas and farces.

name: Victoria Park
searchPreviewText: A large East End park serving the crowded districts around Bethnal Green.

name: W. Tarn & Co.
searchPreviewText: General house furnishers and silk mercers with large departments for furniture, drapery, clothing, and mourning.

name: Wapping Old Stairs
searchPreviewText: Shipping quarter, remembered in Dibdin's river songs.

name: Waterloo Station
searchPreviewText: A major railway terminus south of the Thames.
defaultVisitText: Porters, cabmen, and passengers crowd the station approaches.

name: West Brompton Station
searchPreviewText: Adjoins West London Railway; serves Beaufort House, Walham Green, athletic grounds, and Brompton Cemetery.

name: Westminster Abbey
searchPreviewText: Abbey, school, Parliament, and church crowd this remarkable Westminster prospect.

name: Westminster Bridge Station
searchPreviewText: Serves Abbey, Parliament, Foreign Office, Home Office, Treasury, India Office, Whitehall, Horse Guards, Old Palace Yard, Embankment, Lambeth Bridge, St Thomas's, and Waterloo.

name: Westminster Palace Hotel
searchPreviewText: One of the largest hotels at the Westminster end of town.

name: Wm. T. Cooper
searchPreviewText: Chemist advertising Cantharidine and Rosemary Cream, marking ink, and effervescing lozenges.

name: Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese
searchPreviewText: Old literary tavern near Fleet Street.

name: Zoological Gardens
searchPreviewText: Regent's Park keeps London's zoological gardens and their public attractions.
